description: Phase A6 swipe on browser surfaces (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the bare string form acts on the active tab, the directional object form scrolls with finger-motion semantics (swiping up reveals content below via window.scrollBy), and the point-to-point form performs a real W3C pointer drag. The enhanced-elements page on the group's test server is the target; the exact scroll deltas are unit-tested (test/swipe.test.js), so this spec asserts the real driver accepts both mechanisms.

tests.description: Load a page, swipe with the string form (active tab), the directional object form, and the point-to-point form, then confirm the page is still responsive with a find.
